notes.anchor: your father, a most distinguished man
notes.note: C. Scribonius Curio, consul 76 BC, a prominent orator of the generation before Cicero; he died while his son was away and before the son's later fame was secured. Cicero here mourns the loss of his witness to their friendship.

notes.anchor: May the gods bless your patrimony
notes.note: The patrimony (patrimonium) was the inherited estate; Curio the younger was notorious for squandering his on games and extravagance, which led him into heavy debt and ultimately into Caesar's political orbit.
